questions.question: In digital IC design, what does a 'netlist' primarily represent?
options: The physical layout of the chip, including polygons and layers.
A textual description of the interconnections between logical components (e.g., gates, flip-flops) and their instances.
The high-level behavioral description of the system using languages like C++ or SystemC.
A waveform file showing the timing characteristics and signal values during simulation.
The power consumption report of the circuit under various operating conditions.

questions.question: In computer architecture, what is the primary function of the 'address bus'?
options: To carry data between the CPU and memory or I/O devices for read/write operations.
To transmit control signals that coordinate operations, such as read/write enables or interrupt requests.
To specify the unique memory location or I/O port that the CPU intends to access for data transfer.
To provide power and ground connections to the various components of the system board.
To synchronize the clock signals across different modules within the processor.

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC), what is the primary objective of 'Clock Tree Synthesis' (CTS)?
options: To optimize the placement of standard cells to minimize the overall chip area.
To generate a robust and balanced clock distribution network that ensures minimal clock skew and proper timing to all sequential elements.
To route all the signal nets between placed cells according to design rules and timing constraints.
To perform static timing analysis on the entire design to identify critical paths and timing violations.
To convert the gate-level netlist into a manufacturable layout by adding detailed polygons.

questions.question: In the context of a pipelined processor, what is a 'structural hazard'?
options: When an instruction attempts to read data that a previous instruction has not yet written to a register or memory location.
When the execution path of an instruction depends on the outcome of a previous conditional branch instruction.
When two or more instructions require the same hardware resource (e.g., an ALU, a memory port) at the same time, leading to a conflict.
When the instruction fetch unit encounters an invalid or unsupported instruction opcode.
When a data cache miss occurs, requiring the processor to stall while data is fetched from a slower memory level.

questions.question: In advanced semiconductor manufacturing, what is the primary benefit of using FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor) technology compared to traditional planar CMOS transistors?
options: Significantly reduced manufacturing cost due to simpler lithography processes and fewer masking steps.
Increased leakage current, which enables faster switching speeds at lower operating voltages.
Improved electrostatic control over the transistor channel due to its 3D gate structure, leading to better power efficiency and performance at smaller geometries.
Elimination of the need for an isolation region between adjacent transistors, allowing for higher integration density.
Enhanced immunity to soft errors caused by cosmic rays, making them ideal for space applications.
